methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $110M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Manna Aero is a Series B-stage drone and autonomous delivery company that has raised $110M to date. The valuation landscape for this sector includes 8 private peers (Elroy Air, Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation among them) and no direct public comparables, limiting the breadth of available benchmarking data.

Given data constraints, a stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($110M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $209M. A peer-median approach was not feasible; while 5 same-sector peers exist in the Drones & Autonomous space, insufficient comparable datapoints meeting narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier) precluded reliable cross-sectional analysis.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $178M (low) to $240M (high), with a median of $209M. This range reflects the mechanics of stage-progression modeling applied to a single methodological approach. Key uncertainties include the absence of relevant same-stage peer valuations for direct comparison, limiting the ability to calibrate the 1.9x multiplier to Manna Aero's specific capital efficiency or market position. Revenue scale, unit economics, and regulatory pathway maturity would materially inform tightening this range, but are not reflected in available data.
